approval.reason: Reading CORS-protected responses from another origin requires user consent.
Submitting OAuth client secrets requires the user to confirm the configuration.
Running an OAuth/browser sign-in flow and storing the result requires the user to authorize the connection.
Disabling a client config breaks new connections and refreshes for that provider.
Collecting a secret from the user and storing it requires explicit user input.

approval.when: caller is not already granted use of the matched credential
approval.reason: Handing a stored credential to an agent for use requires the user to authorize it.
Persisting a credential the agent supplied requires the user to approve it.
Opening URLs in the system browser from code requires user consent.

service.description: Authenticated clients and the product host control runtime leases

approval.when: cloning another, already-existing context than the caller
approval.reason: cloning another agent or panel's existing context state requires approval

restrictedTo.reason: session entities are host-managed, except a launch-orchestrator extension may create a source-tagged session

approval.when: launching into another, already-existing context than the caller
approval.reason: launching code into another agent or panel's existing context requires approval

approval.when: destroying another agent or panel's existing context (not one you own)
approval.reason: destroying another agent or panel's existing context requires approval
